# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-27
**Delivered:** 12:11 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Global markets enter a pivotal week dominated by two interlocking forces: the ongoing US-Iran war and its associated Strait of Hormuz closure, and the most consequential earnings week of 2026, with five of the Magnificent 7 reporting. Oil prices remain elevated above $100/bbl as peace talks have stalled again after President Trump confirmed no US envoys would be dispatched to Pakistan for negotiations, while Goldman Sachs has raised its Brent Q4 forecast to $90/bbl citing "extreme" inventory draws from the prolonged Hormuz closure [Source: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/goldman-hikes-oil-price-forecasts-on-extreme-inventory-draws]. The **single most important variable today** is the FOMC decision on April 29, which will almost certainly be Jerome Powell's last meeting as Fed Chair before Kevin Warsh takes over — the accompanying statement and press conference guidance on the stagflation trade-off will be market-moving. US consumer sentiment has collapsed to a record low of 49.8, below levels seen during the financial crisis and COVID, as inflation fears tied to the Iran war intensify [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/consumer-sentiment-at-record-low-as-americans-feel-impact-of-war-in-iran-142920073.html].

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The macro landscape in late April 2026 is defined by a war-driven stagflationary shock of historic proportions. The US-Iran conflict, now eight weeks old, has effectively shuttered the Strait of Hormuz — a chokepoint that normally handles approximately 20-21 million barrels per day of crude, representing roughly one-fifth of all petroleum traded globally [Source: https://discoveryalert.com.au/strait-hormuz-closure-us-iran-talks-oil-supply-crisis/]. Bypass infrastructure covers only an estimated 35-40% of normal Hormuz flows at maximum utilisation, leaving a supply gap exceeding 10 million barrels per day. Global visible oil inventories have declined approximately 255 million barrels since the start of the conflict, with Goldman Sachs warning inventories could reach all-time lows even if the Strait reopens by late April [Source: https://www.icis.com/chemicals-and-the-economy/2026/04/imf-warns-on-inflation-and-recession-risks-as-iran-war-continues/].

Despite the energy shock, equity markets have shown remarkable resilience. The S&P 500 closed at 7,165.08 (+0.80%) and the Nasdaq 100 at 27,303.67 (+1.95%) on Friday April 24, with both indices recording fresh record highs, driven by a massive semiconductor rally led by Intel's earnings beat [Source: https://www.marketwatch.com/livecoverage/s-p-500-dow-jones-nasdaq-donald-trump-cease-fire-extended-israel-lebanon-3-weeks]. The Dow Jones, at 49,230.71 (-0.16%), lagged due to the weight of elevated oil prices on transportation and consumer discretionary names [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/^DJI]. This divergence underscores the market's current tech-driven character: energy pain is being partially offset by AI/semiconductor enthusiasm, while mega-cap tech earnings this week (Alphabet, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft Wednesday; Apple Thursday) are expected to show blended S&P 500 earnings growth of 15.1% YoY [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/25/what-investors-should-watch-in-the-big-tech-earnings-week-ahead/].

The Federal Reserve meeting on April 28-29 is universally expected to result in a rate hold, with the Fed Funds Rate steady at 3.50-3.75% (effective rate 3.64%) [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS]. Fed officials have explicitly adopted a "wait-and-see" posture, watching how the Iran war is affecting the economy before moving in either direction. This is widely expected to be Powell's last meeting as Chair before Kevin Warsh is confirmed — the DOJ recently ended its investigation into Powell, clearing the path for Warsh's confirmation [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/fed-likely-to-hold-rates-steady-april-consumer-impact.html]. Markets will closely parse whether the statement acknowledges the stagflation risk explicitly.

Inflation dynamics are deteriorating. CPI stands at 3.32% YoY as of March [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL] while PPI MoM printed +1.78% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/PPIACO]. More alarmingly, University of Michigan year-ahead inflation expectations jumped to 4.7% in April from 3.8% in March, coinciding with the record-low consumer sentiment reading of 49.8 [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/consumer-sentiment-at-record-low-as-americans-feel-impact-of-war-in-iran-142920073.html]. The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.34% and 2-year at 3.83%, producing a positive 51bp term premium as bond traders brace for a potential war-driven inflation shock this week across every G7 central bank meeting [Source: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-26/bond-traders-eye-packed-week-of-rate-decisions-for-sell-signals].

The IMF has issued formal warnings on both inflation and recession risks stemming from the Iran war, noting the U.S. has been relatively spared compared to other nations — particularly in Europe and Asia — which are feeling the full brunt of the energy shock through longer tanker rerouting times [Source: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/27/business/economy/iran-war-global-growth.html]. The DXY index trades at 98.318, reflecting a USD that has softened against most G10 currencies despite elevated yields, a sign that safe-haven premium is flowing towards gold ($4,718.50) and oil-linked currencies rather than the dollar [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/DX-Y.NYB].

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

**US-Iran War & Strait of Hormuz Closure**

- Iran has offered a new proposal to the US for reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, while shelving nuclear talks to a later date, according to a US official and two sources cited by Axios. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/us-iran-peace-talks-stall-global-markets-stocks-oil-treasurys.html]
- US-Iran peace talks stalled over the weekend after President Trump confirmed he would not be sending US envoys to Pakistan for negotiations, causing oil prices to climb more than 2% Monday. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-27-2026]
- The Strait of Hormuz handles approximately one-fifth of all global petroleum trade (~20-21 million bpd). Existing bypass infrastructure covers only 35-40% of normal flows at maximum utilisation, leaving a supply gap of more than 10 million bpd that cannot be bridged through routing alternatives. [Source: https://discoveryalert.com.au/strait-hormuz-closure-us-iran-talks-oil-supply-crisis/]
- Global visible oil inventories have declined by 255 million barrels since the start of the conflict and are likely to reach all-time lows even if the Strait reopens by late April, according to Goldman Sachs data cited by ICIS. [Source: https://www.icis.com/chemicals-and-the-economy/2026/04/imf-warns-on-inflation-and-recession-risks-as-iran-war-continues/]
- Goldman Sachs raised its Brent Q4 2026 forecast to $90/bbl (from $80) due to "extreme" inventory draws from the prolonged Hormuz closure. WTI Q4 forecasts were also raised. [Source: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/goldman-hikes-oil-price-forecasts-on-extreme-inventory-draws]
- Iran's foreign minister may arrive in Pakistan tonight with a second round of US-Iran talks expected, per multiple reports, providing a tentative de-escalation signal. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/24/amazon-to-provide-meta-with-hundreds-of-thousands-of-aws-ai-chips-in-new-deal.html]
- Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama issued a verbal warning on FX intervention, saying authorities could take "decisive" action against speculative moves, noting that past interventions had been effective. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-24/world-stocks-oil-edge-us-iran-deadlock-weighs]

## 8. WEEK AHEAD / SCENARIO ANALYSIS

| SCENARIO | PROBABILITY | TRIGGER | BRENT | S&P 500 | BTC |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|-----|
| Bull | 25% | Iran FM reaches deal in Pakistan; Hormuz reopening timeline confirmed; Mag-7 earnings beat consensus; Fed hold with dovish tone on future cuts | $88 | 7,450 | $85,000 |
| Base | 55% | Iran talks stall but no escalation; Fed holds with neutral statement; Mag-7 earnings broadly in-line; oil stays $95-105 range | $100 | 7,200 | $78,000 |
| Bear | 20% | Iran talks collapse with fresh military escalation; FOMC signals hawkish hold citing war inflation; Mag-7 miss guidance; Brent spikes above $110 | $112 | 6,800 | $68,000 |

**Key Upcoming Catalysts This Week:**

1. **April 28-29 — FOMC Meeting & Press Conference (April 29):** Almost certainly Powell's final meeting as Fed Chair. Rate hold universally expected, but the statement's language on stagflation risk and the balance of risks between inflation and employment will be the key market-mover. Fed Funds rate at 3.64% effective with 10Y at 4.34%. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/fed-likely-to-hold-rates-steady-april-consumer-impact.html]

2. **April 29 — Wednesday Mega-Cap Tech Earnings (After Close):** Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta (META), Amazon (AMZN), and Microsoft (MSFT) all report after the bell. S&P 500 blended earnings growth is running at 15.1% YoY — these four results will confirm or deny that pace. [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/25/what-investors-should-watch-in-the-big-tech-earnings-week-ahead/]

3. **April 30 — Apple (AAPL) Earnings (After Close):** The final Magnificent 7 report of the week; Apple's consumer-facing business makes it the most direct read-through to consumer health, critical given record-low consumer sentiment at 49.8. [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/25/what-investors-should-watch-in-the-big-tech-earnings-week-ahead/]

4. **Week of April 27 — Bank of Japan, Bank of England, European Central Bank, Bank of Canada Rate Decisions:** Every G7 central bank is meeting this week — a historically rare occurrence. Bond traders are watching for fresh sell signals as policymakers confront the war-driven inflation shock. [Source: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-26/bond-traders-eye-packed-week-of-rate-decisions-for-sell-signals]

5. **Ongoing — US-Iran Peace Talks / Hormuz Status:** Iran's FM is expected to arrive in Pakistan tonight for a second round of talks. Any credible Hormuz reopening timeline would be the single biggest intraweek market catalyst, sending oil sharply lower and risk assets sharply higher. Conversely, a breakdown risks Brent spiking above $110. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/us-iran-peace-talks-stall-global-markets-stocks-oil-treasurys.html]

---
